passage.title: Food Drive Check
passage.text: Our community club tested two ways to sort food-drive donations last month. Lina used a single big basket, but Noah used color donation bins. After three collection days, Noah's team made fewer mistakes because the color bins were easy to spot. The club counted each donation and checked where it was placed. When Lina saw the difference, she changed to color donation bins. Two days later, her group needed less help from the teacher. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that small sorting boxes can improve community work.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Testing which system helps in a food drive
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Noah's team use to sort the donations?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does donation mean in the first sentence?
choices: A person who visits a club
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Lina switch to color donation bins?
choices: The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
She thought color bins would help the group work faster
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
